topic.angle: You don't need a gym membership. A 20-minute walk genuinely outperforms most antidepressants for mild depression.

hook: Stop Saying Exercise Doesn't Help Depression

slides.title: I need the gym
slides.body: Reframe: A 20-minute walk works. Research shows it matches antidepressants for mild depression—no membership needed.

slides.title: I'm too tired
slides.body: Reframe: Movement creates energy. Even 10 minutes outside breaks the fatigue cycle better than scrolling in bed.

slides.title: It has to be intense
slides.body: Reframe: Gentle movement counts. Walking to the corner shop, stretching at your desk—it all shifts your brain chemistry.

slides.title: I don't have time
slides.body: Reframe: You have 15 minutes. Park further away. Take the stairs. Mental health doesn't need an hour.

slides.title: Exercise is punishment
slides.body: Reframe: Movement is medicine. It's not about fixing your body. It's about calming your mind when nothing else will.

cta.title: Save this. Send to a friend who needs it.
cta.body: 24/7 voice-first mental health support — link in bio. 🔗

caption_body: You don't need to run marathons or lift heavy. A 20-minute walk around your block genuinely outperforms most antidepressants for mild depression—that's not motivation talk, that's peer-reviewed research. The problem isn't that movement doesn't work. It's that we've been sold the idea it needs to hurt, cost money, or take an hour. It doesn't. Start smaller than feels worth it. Walk to the end of your street. Stretch while the kettle boils. Your brain doesn't care if it's optimised—it just needs you to move. → Link in bio for MindCore AI. 7-day trial €1.99.
full_caption:
You don't need to run marathons or lift heavy. A 20-minute walk around your block genuinely outperforms most antidepressants for mild depression—that's not motivation talk, that's peer-reviewed research. The problem isn't that movement doesn't work. It's that we've been sold the idea it needs to hurt, cost money, or take an hour. It doesn't. Start smaller than feels worth it. Walk to the end of your street. Stretch while the kettle boils. Your brain doesn't care if it's optimised—it just needs you to move. → Link in bio for MindCore AI. 7-day trial €1.99.
